canonical_definition: The provided material characterizes headline generation as the observable task of producing a concise and relevant title for a given body of text. This behavior is framed as a supervised task, with one study noting a lack of relevant corpora for it. To operationalize this task, models are fine-tuned on specialized datasets, such as the CMHG dataset which was designed for Tibetan, Uyghur, and Mongolian. Performance is then evaluated using high-quality annotated data. The ROUGE-L score is used as a metric to assess whether the models can generate "concise and contextually accurate headlines."
papers: RAED: Retrieval-Augmented Entity Description Generation for Emerging Entity Linking and Disambiguation
Sticker-TTS: Learn to Utilize Historical Experience with a Sticker-driven Test-Time Scaling Framework
Table-LLM-Specialist: Language Model Specialists for Tables using Iterative Fine-tuning
